title: Lastrea confusa
intro: Lastrea confusa, also known as the confused lady fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Athyriaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea confusa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Lastrea confusa is often used to make tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Lastrea confusa is classified as a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It belongs to the genus Lastrea, which is a group of ferns that are native to the Old World. Within the genus, Lastrea confusa is one of several species that are closely related and share similar characteristics. The classification of Lastrea confusa is based on its morphological features, such as its frond shape and venation patterns. This classification helps to identify and distinguish Lastrea confusa from other related fern species.
